heroHeadline: US Navy Seizes Iranian Ship MV Touska; Iran Pulls Out of Islamabad Talks Round 2 as Ceasefire Expires in 48 Hours
heroSubtitle: The USS Spruance fired on and seized the Iranian cargo ship MV Touska near the Strait of Hormuz, prompting Iran to call it 'armed piracy' and withdraw from the second round of Islamabad peace talks. Oil prices surged — Brent +6.3% to $96, WTI +7% to $88.3 — as the US-Iran ceasefire counts down to its April 22 expiration with no deal in sight. Iran's FM Araghchi cited US 'bad intentions'; VP Aref called US demands 'childish.' Xi Jinping urged Hormuz remain open, while the Lebanon-Israel ceasefire also expires April 22.
footerNote: Data updated 2026-04-20. Sources include CENTCOM, IDF, IRNA, IAEA, Hengaw, HRANA, Al Jazeera, Anadolu Agency, Reuters, Associated Press, CNN, CNBC, Bloomberg, IEA, NPR, CBS News, ABC News, NBC News, PBS NewsHour, Xinhua, Euronews, CFR, PRIO.
